## Summary
Investigated why Claude Code's stop hooks were slow and blocking on session exit. Found that Cairn's `session-summary` Stop hook was spawning a full recursive Claude call (with 120s timeout) to generate session summaries, consuming 30s–2min per turn. Disabled the Cairn plugin entirely to resolve the bottleneck.

## Extraction Candidates
- **Cairn Stop hook slow-down** (source-summary) — Cairn's session-summary hook calls `claude -p --model haiku` recursively on transcript truncation, blocking the CLI with a 120s timeout. Disabling the plugin removes it; lowering timeout or running async are alternatives.

content.text:
Now I have the live picture. Here's the walkthrough.

---

## How it works (in 30 seconds)

Three hooks: **SessionStart** + **PostCompact** run `hooks/inject` (bash, 109 lines) which reads your vault under a 2KB budget and emits `additionalContext` JSON to Claude Code. **Stop** runs `cairn capture-session` (TS) which writes a tiny manifest (no LLM at capture time). Summarization is lazy: `cairn summarize` calls `claude -p --model haiku` on demand, caches in `sessions/summaries/`.

That's the architecture. It's fine. The implementation has issues though.

---

## Injection correctness — what's actually wrong

**1. The 2KB budget is comically too small for your vault.**

`index.md` is 20KB on your machine. The inject hook reads it second (after `context.md`), and you don't *have* `context.md`, so index.md is read first. It alone is 10× the budget. The hook keeps appending sections "if they fit" — none fits. Result: Claude gets the first ~2KB of your index, no sessions at all.

You can verify this directly:

```bash
CAIRN_VAULT=~/cairn ~/Projects/cairn/hooks/inject | jq -r '.hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext' | wc -c
```

I'd bet it's right at 2048 ± a few bytes, all index, no sessions. Your "recent sessions in context" feature has been silently disabled by the budget.

**2. The "verify against codebase" disclaimer is gated on the wrong condition.**

Look at `hooks/inject:46-91`. The header `## Cairn Vault Context\n\nVerify against codebase before acting on any recalled facts.` is only emitted if `context.md` OR `index.md` exists. If a vault has only `sessions/` (e.g., very early state, or a future user who hasn't built up the index yet), the injected context starts with `### Recent Sessions` and the disclaimer never appears. That's a real-world possibility — `cairn init` doesn't create non-empty `context.md` or `index.md`, so a fresh vault hits this case.

**3. JSON escaping handles only `\n`, `\r`, `\t` — not other control chars.**

`escape_for_json` in `hooks/inject:94-102` does:
```bash
s="${s//\\/\\\\}"
s="${s//\"/\\\"}"
s="${s//$'\n'/\\n}"
s="${s//$'\r'/\\r}"
s="${s//$'\t'/\\t}"
```

But JSON requires escaping all U+0000–U+001F. If a vault file contains a NUL byte, form feed, vertical tab, or any other control char (e.g., from a paste of weird content, an accidentally-binary file in `wiki/`, or a Windows-y document with `\f`), the emitted JSON is invalid. Claude Code rejects the hook output and you get *no* context injected. Silent failure mode.

**4. Vault content with `<system-reminder>` tags gets injected back into context.**

I just `cat`-ed `~/cairn/sessions/2026-04-22T19-02-51.md` and it contains a literal `<system-reminder>` block (captured from a prior Claude transcript that hit your "stale task tools" reminder). The hook reinjects that text verbatim. Claude Code may treat injected `<system-reminder>` text as a real reminder, or render it inertly — depends on how additionalContext is processed downstream. Either way, vault content can carry control sequences (XML-ish tags, `[!info]` callouts, `<thinking>` blocks) that have meaning to Claude Code or to Claude itself. Worth thinking about whether to sanitize.

**5. Byte counting math is actually correct.** I traced it carefully and was tempted to flag a bug — `printf '%b'` interprets `\n` escapes which made me suspect the count was off — but it works out, because the same `printf '%b'` runs at output time before JSON escaping, so the budget reflects what gets emitted. False alarm.

---

## Frontmatter — drift and sediment

**1. You have three different session schemas in your vault simultaneously.**

- 122 files match the old "Prompt is too long" pattern (failed haiku summarization wrote the error string as the session record — the bug v0.6.0 fixed)
- ~100 files have a *second* legacy schema: YAML frontmatter with `session_id` as a timestamp string, plus `status`, `extracted`, `decisions`, `open_threads`, `files_changed`, plus a markdown body with `## Summary` and `## Extraction Candidates`. This is what your previous Stop hook wrote.
- Zero files match the new `SessionManifest` schema (filename `<ts>-<short_id>.md` with `transcript_path`, `transcript_hash`, `excerpt`, etc.).

Migration was built. Migration hasn't been run. `cairn migrate-sessions --apply --yes` is a single command and it's the right move.

**2. The new SessionManifest exposes raw machinery to the human reader.**

When migration does run, manifests will surface fields like:
```yaml
extracted: false
decisions: []
open_threads: []
tags: []
```

Default-empty fields are written *every time* even when never used. None of these are documented in `templates/CAIRN.md` or `skills/cairn/SKILL.md`. A human (or Claude) opening a session manifest sees four empty arrays with cryptic names and no schema doc. Either drop the defaults from the serialized output (only emit when non-empty) or add a "Session Manifest Schema" section to CAIRN.md.

**3. `transcript_size` is hashed but redundant with `transcript_hash`.**

`computeManifestHash` includes `transcript_size`. The hash is over file content, so size is implicit. Including it adds invalidation surface (a bit-flip-only change to the file would change the hash anyway, but writing a file with identical content and identical size confirms the hash is sufficient). Minor.

**4. Wiki-page schema is consistent across surfaces.** I cross-checked `templates/CAIRN.md` ↔ `skills/cairn/SKILL.md`. The five page types and the required fields agree. `source` is correctly required for `source-summary` and present in the per-type template (I had hallucinated drift here earlier; there isn't any).

---

## Budget sizing — your actual data

| Slot | Your bytes | Budget | Fits? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `context.md` | (file doesn't exist) | first slot | n/a |
| `index.md` | 20,445 | 2,048 | no — only first ~2KB |
| `sessions/` (newest first) | typically 1–3KB each | rest | **none fit** |

Practical implication: with your current vault, the recall purpose of injection is dead. Claude sees a truncated index and no session context, so the "compaction-resilient memory" pitch is silently broken.

Possible fixes, ordered by impact:
- **Bump the default to 8KB or 16KB.** Modern context windows easily absorb that. The original 2KB feels like a Karpathy-esque deliberate constraint, but in practice it starves the feature.
- **Or: change the injection strategy from "fits-in-budget verbatim" to "summary-of-index + 1-2 most recent session summaries."** The index gets pre-digested into a top-N categorized digest at write time (when `index.md` is updated), and the inject hook reads the digest, not the raw index.
- **Either way: maintain `context.md`.** It's the highest-priority slot and you don't have one. The "Working Set" pattern only works if you actually use it.

---

## What I'd actually do, if it were my vault

1. Run `cairn migrate-sessions --apply --yes` — collapse the three schemas into one.
2. Bump `CAIRN_BUDGET` default to 8192 in `src/lib/constants.ts`. Two-line change.
3. Fix the "verify against codebase" disclaimer to always emit when any context is appended — move it into the `escape_for_json` step, not gated on file existence.
4. Add the JSON control-char escape (one regex pass over `[\x00-\x1f]` after the existing replacements).
5. Decide: do you actually use `context.md`? If yes, build a habit. If no, drop the priority-1 slot and move sessions up.

The schema-cross-check, the frontmatter audit, the seven-agent fan-out — none of that surfaces issues bigger than these five. The budget is the load-bearing finding; everything else is polish.

What do you want to dig into?
On it. Doing the safe code changes first, then the migration last.
Let me check how `cairn` is invoked from this checkout, then make the code changes.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cairn injection hook — SessionStart and PostCompact
# Reads vault in priority order: context.md → index.md → recent sessions.
# Outputs JSON for Claude Code context injection.

set -euo pipefail

# Resolve vault path: argument > env > default
VAULT_PATH="${1:-${CAIRN_VAULT:-${HOME}/cairn}}"
BUDGET="${CAIRN_BUDGET:-8192}"

# Graceful degradation: missing vault = empty context
if [ ! -d "$VAULT_PATH" ]; then
  printf '{\n  "hookSpecificOutput": {\n    "hookEventName": "SessionStart",\n    "additionalContext": ""\n  }\n}\n'
  exit 0
fi

context=""
current_bytes=0

# Utility: get byte count of a string
byte_count() {
  printf '%b' "$1" | wc -c | tr -d ' '
}

# Utility: append to context if within budget
append_if_fits() {
  local section="$1"
  local candidate
  if [ -z "$context" ]; then
    candidate="$section"
  else
    candidate="${context}\n\n${section}"
  fi
  local candidate_bytes
  candidate_bytes=$(byte_count "$candidate")
  if [ "$candidate_bytes" -le "$BUDGET" ]; then
    context="$candidate"
    current_bytes=$candidate_bytes
    return 0
  fi
  return 1
}

# Emit a section. On the first emission, prepend the vault-context disclaimer
# header so it is present whenever any vault content is injected — not only
# when context.md or index.md happen to exist.
emit_section() {
  local section="$1"
  if [ -z "$context" ]; then
    append_if_fits "## Cairn Vault Context\n\nVerify against codebase before acting on any recalled facts.\n\n${section}"
  else
    append_if_fits "$section"
  fi
}

# 1. context.md (working set) — highest priority
CONTEXT_FILE="${VAULT_PATH}/context.md"
if [ -f "$CONTEXT_FILE" ]; then
  context_content=$(cat "$CONTEXT_FILE")
  emit_section "### Working Set\n${context_content}" || true
fi

# 2. index.md — second priority
INDEX_FILE="${VAULT_PATH}/index.md"
if [ -f "$INDEX_FILE" ]; then
  index_content=$(cat "$INDEX_FILE")
  emit_section "### Index\n${index_content}" || true
fi

# 3. Recent sessions (newest first, by filename sort)
SESSIONS_DIR="${VAULT_PATH}/sessions"
if [ -d "$SESSIONS_DIR" ]; then
  session_files=$(ls -r "$SESSIONS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$session_files" ]; then
    sessions_header_added=false
    for f in $session_files; do
      base_name=$(basename "$f")
      summary_file="${SESSIONS_DIR}/summaries/${base_name}"
      if [ -f "$summary_file" ]; then
        session_content=$(cat "$summary_file")
      else
        session_content=$(cat "$f")
      fi
      if [ "$sessions_header_added" = false ]; then
        section="### Recent Sessions\n---\n${session_content}"
        sessions_header_added=true
      else
        section="---\n${session_content}"
      fi
      emit_section "$section" || break
    done
  fi
fi

# 4. Entire status indicator (one line if enabled)
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1 && entire status 2>/dev/null | grep -q "Enabled"; then
  emit_section "### Entire\nEntire is enabled in this project. Session manifests may have \`entire_checkpoint\` fields for richer extraction via \`entire explain\`." || true
fi

# Escape for JSON. Order matters: backslash substitution must run before any
# substitution that introduces backslashes. Strips remaining ASCII control
# characters (0x00-0x07, 0x0B, 0x0E-0x1F) that JSON cannot represent
# unescaped — they should not appear in vault markdown but a malformed paste
# or accidental binary would otherwise break the JSON output.
escape_for_json() {
  local s="$1"
  s="${s//\\/\\\\}"
  s="${s//\"/\\\"}"
  s="${s//$'\n'/\\n}"
  s="${s//$'\r'/\\r}"
  s="${s//$'\t'/\\t}"
  s="${s//$'\b'/\\b}"
  s="${s//$'\f'/\\f}"
  printf '%s' "$s" | LC_ALL=C tr -d '\000-\007\013\016-\037'
}

escaped=$(escape_for_json "$(printf '%b' "$context")")

# Output JSON — Claude Code format
printf '{\n  "hookSpecificOutput": {\n    "hookEventName": "SessionStart",\n    "additionalContext": "%s"\n  }\n}\n' "$escaped"

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cairn injection hook — SessionStart and PostCompact
# Reads vault in priority order: context.md → index.md → recent sessions.
# Outputs JSON for Claude Code context injection.

set -euo pipefail

# Resolve vault path: argument > env > default
VAULT_PATH="${1:-${CAIRN_VAULT:-${HOME}/cairn}}"
BUDGET="${CAIRN_BUDGET:-2048}"

# Graceful degradation: missing vault = empty context
if [ ! -d "$VAULT_PATH" ]; then
  printf '{\n  "hookSpecificOutput": {\n    "hookEventName": "SessionStart",\n    "additionalContext": ""\n  }\n}\n'
  exit 0
fi

context=""
current_bytes=0

# Utility: get byte count of a string
byte_count() {
  printf '%b' "$1" | wc -c | tr -d ' '
}

# Utility: append to context if within budget
append_if_fits() {
  local section="$1"
  local candidate
  if [ -z "$context" ]; then
    candidate="$section"
  else
    candidate="${context}\n\n${section}"
  fi
  local candidate_bytes
  candidate_bytes=$(byte_count "$candidate")
  if [ "$candidate_bytes" -le "$BUDGET" ]; then
    context="$candidate"
    current_bytes=$candidate_bytes
    return 0
  fi
  return 1
}

# 1. context.md (working set) — highest priority
CONTEXT_FILE="${VAULT_PATH}/context.md"
if [ -f "$CONTEXT_FILE" ]; then
  context_content=$(cat "$CONTEXT_FILE")
  append_if_fits "## Cairn Vault Context\n\nVerify against codebase before acting on any recalled facts.\n\n### Working Set\n${context_content}" || true
fi

# 2. index.md — second priority
INDEX_FILE="${VAULT_PATH}/index.md"
if [ -f "$INDEX_FILE" ]; then
  index_content=$(cat "$INDEX_FILE")
  if [ -z "$context" ]; then
    append_if_fits "## Cairn Vault Context\n\nVerify against codebase before acting on any recalled facts.\n\n### Index\n${index_content}" || true
  else
    append_if_fits "### Index\n${index_content}" || true
  fi
fi

# 3. Recent sessions (newest first, by filename sort)
SESSIONS_DIR="${VAULT_PATH}/sessions"
if [ -d "$SESSIONS_DIR" ]; then
  session_files=$(ls -r "$SESSIONS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$session_files" ]; then
    sessions_header_added=false
    for f in $session_files; do
      base_name=$(basename "$f")
      summary_file="${SESSIONS_DIR}/summaries/${base_name}"
      if [ -f "$summary_file" ]; then
        session_content=$(cat "$summary_file")
      else
        session_content=$(cat "$f")
      fi
      if [ "$sessions_header_added" = false ]; then
        section="\n### Recent Sessions\n---\n${session_content}"
        sessions_header_added=true
      else
        section="\n---\n${session_content}"
      fi
      append_if_fits "$section" || break
    done
  fi
fi

# 4. Entire status indicator (one line if enabled)
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1 && entire status 2>/dev/null | grep -q "Enabled"; then
  append_if_fits "\n### Entire\nEntire is enabled in this project. Session manifests may have \`entire_checkpoint\` fields for richer extraction via \`entire explain\`." || true
fi

# Escape for JSON
escape_for_json() {
  local s="$1"
  s="${s//\\/\\\\}"
  s="${s//\"/\\\"}"
  s="${s//$'\n'/\\n}"
  s="${s//$'\r'/\\r}"
  s="${s//$'\t'/\\t}"
  printf '%s' "$s"
}

escaped=$(escape_for_json "$(printf '%b' "$context")")

# Output JSON — Claude Code format
printf '{\n  "hookSpecificOutput": {\n    "hookEventName": "SessionStart",\n    "additionalContext": "%s"\n  }\n}\n' "$escaped"

exit 0

Extended lookahead to detect digit+`\<non-letter>` and digit+non-alphanumeric+closing-`$` on same line. Locks in specs for `$800°C$`, `$13\%$`, `$1650°C$`, `$99.999\%$`.

content.text:
Done. Here's what landed:

**Code changes** (`/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/cairn`):
- `src/lib/constants.ts`: `DEFAULT_BUDGET 2048 → 8192`
- `hooks/inject`: budget default now 8192; disclaimer header refactored into `emit_section` so it always appears when any vault content is injected (not gated on context.md/index.md existing); `escape_for_json` extended with `\b`/`\f` and a `tr -d` strip pass for remaining control chars
- `src/lib/migration.ts`: new `malformed-yaml` session class wrapping `parseFrontmatter` in try/catch — one file killed the whole migration; routed to `.trash/`
- `tests/inject.test.ts`: budget test pinned to `CAIRN_BUDGET=2048` explicitly so it tests budget-respect, not the default value

**Migration of `~/cairn/sessions`** (224 files):
- 183 legacy-well-formed → manifests + cached summaries
- 33 legacy-error ("Prompt is too long") → `.trash/`
- 1 malformed-yaml (your LaTeX session with bad `\\letter` escapes) → `.trash/`
- 7 unknown-class legacy files remain in place; `cairn doctor` flags them but they don't break anything

**Verification:**
- `bun test` — 111/111 pass
- `bunx tsc --noEmit` — clean
- `bun run src/cli.ts doctor` — vault healthy, manifests and summaries 1:1
- Live hook output now: 8,008 bytes injected, "Recent Sessions" section populated with real summary content (e.g. the "Cairn Stop hook slow-down" session). Before today's change you were getting only truncated index, no sessions.

**Three things worth flagging before you walk away:**

1. **Your `index.md` (20KB) still doesn't fit, even at 8KB budget.** `emit_section` is all-or-nothing per section. So the hook silently skips index.md and moves to sessions. You're getting 8KB of session recall but zero index. If you want index visible too, the fix is either truncate index.md (it's grown unwieldy — 175 wiki pages × ~80 chars/entry will always be 14KB+) or have the hook truncate the index to fit. Worth thinking about; not done here.

2. **Vault content includes literal `<system-reminder>` tags** (captured from prior Claude transcripts, e.g. the "task tools haven't been used recently" reminder showed up verbatim in your sessions). The hook reinjects those. Claude Code may treat them as real reminders. Consider stripping them in `cairn capture-session` before writing the excerpt — one regex against `<system-reminder>...</system-reminder>`.

3. **`context.md` is still empty.** That's the highest-priority slot and it's unused. Whether to maintain a working set is a workflow decision, not a code one — your call.

Want me to commit these changes? They're a coherent set: "fix: enlarge inject budget, always emit disclaimer, tolerate malformed YAML in migration." Or pick them apart into separate commits if you want narrower scope.

Three coupled changes to hooks/inject and the constant it reads:

1. Default CAIRN_BUDGET 2048 → 8192. The 2KB budget was too small for
   any vault past ~25 wiki pages — index.md alone routinely exceeded
   it, leaving zero bytes for session context. 8KB lets recent
   session summaries actually fit.

2. The "## Cairn Vault Context" disclaimer was gated on context.md or
   index.md existing. A vault with only sessions/ would emit content
   with no header and no "verify against codebase" notice. Refactored
   into emit_section so the header always appears when any vault
   content is injected.

3. JSON escaping handled only \n, \r, \t. Other ASCII control chars
   (\b, \f, NUL, etc.) would produce invalid JSON if a vault file
   contained them. Added \b/\f escapes and a tr -d strip for the
   remaining control bytes.

The budget-respect test now sets CAIRN_BUDGET=2048 explicitly so it
verifies budget enforcement rather than the specific default value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status

input.command:
git add src/lib/migration.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: tolerate malformed YAML in session migration

classifySessionFile called parseFrontmatter without a try/catch, so a
single legacy file with invalid YAML escapes (e.g., haiku output that
emitted \\letter inside a double-quoted string) would throw out of
buildMigrationPlan and abort the whole migration before any other
file was processed.

Wrap the parse in try/catch and add a malformed-yaml session class
that routes to sessions/.trash/, parallel to legacy-error. The user
sees the bad files distinguished from "Prompt is too long" cases in
the migration plan output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -5
